metadata.summary: Python patterns to create DISPLAY_NETWORK campaigns, add contextual+audience targeting, build responsive display ads, and query placement performance.

content.title: Google Display Ads — Campaign Setup, Targeting, Responsive Ads
content.summary: Python patterns for creating DISPLAY_NETWORK campaigns, adding contextual (keyword/placement) and audience targeting to ad groups, building responsive display ads with required asset sets (1-5 headlines, long headline, 1-5 descriptions, 1-15 images), and querying placement-level performance reports.

why.p: Display campaigns require explicit network_settings flags to avoid search spill-over. Responsive display ads have mandatory asset minimums and optional logo slots; submitting without the required fields causes API validation errors that are not obvious from the error message alone.

list.item: Creating banner ad campaigns across Google Display Network (GDN)
Adding remarketing audiences or contextual keyword targeting to display ad groups
Uploading image assets and assembling responsive display ads (RDA)
Reporting on which placements are driving impressions and conversions
Migrating legacy image ads to RDAs (Google sunsets uploaded display ads in favour of RDA)
Search text ads — use google-search-ads methodology instead
Performance Max campaigns — use google-pmax methodology instead
Shopping product listing ads — use google-shopping-ads methodology instead
YouTube-only video campaigns — use google-video-ads methodology instead

section.name: trigger-conditions
section.p: The agent should activate this methodology when ANY of the following signals appear in the task or codebase context.

list.item: Task text mentions: "display ads", "GDN", "responsive display", "banner campaign", "remarketing", "RDA"
Code references: AdvertisingChannelType.DISPLAY, ResponsiveDisplayAdInfo, AssetService, detail_placement_view, user_list
Existing campaign has advertising_channel_type == DISPLAY and the agent is asked to modify targeting, creatives, or budget
A request for "display performance report" or "placement exclusions"

checklist.check: OAuth refresh token + developer token loaded; GOOGLE_ADS_CONFIGURATION_FILE_PATH env var points to valid google-ads.yaml
customer_id is the leaf account (10 digits, no dashes); login_customer_id is the MCC manager id if applicable
Conversion tracking already configured (otherwise display ROAS reporting is meaningless)
At least one existing user_list (for audience targeting) or topic/keyword inventory researched

rule.statement: Set target_content_network=True and target_google_search=False when creating a Display campaign. The default allows search network exposure, which mixes display and search traffic in the same campaign.
rule.rationale: Search and display have different CPCs, CTRs, and conversion rates. Mixing them in one campaign makes performance analysis unreliable and wastes budget on unintended placements.

list.item: target_google_search: False — disable Google Search results
target_search_network: False — disable Google Search partner sites
target_partner_search_network: False — disable search partner network
target_content_network: True — enable Google Display Network
Contextual: target by keywords, topics, or specific placements (URLs/apps)
Audience: target custom audiences, affinity segments, or in-market segments
Demographics: age, gender, parental status, household income
Remarketing: show ads to previous site visitors based on behavior (user_list)
Customer Match: hashed email/phone uploads (SHA-256 of normalized lowercase value)

rule.statement: Layer at most 2 targeting types per ad group for the initial campaign. Over-constraining with 3+ layers severely limits reach and makes it impossible to diagnose which layer is blocking delivery.
rule.rationale: Multiple targeting layers apply AND logic — each additional layer reduces the eligible audience multiplicatively.

list.item: DISPLAY_STANDARD: standard display ads with targeting
DISPLAY_UPLOAD_ADS: custom uploaded image or HTML5 ads (legacy; prefer RDA)

how-to.step: 1. Create a Campaign Budget. Set delivery_method=STANDARD for steady pacing or ACCELERATED only for short-burst tests.
2. Create a Display Campaign with advertising_channel_type=DISPLAY, status=PAUSED (always start paused), manual_cpc or target_cpa bidding, and the network_settings rules above.
3. Disable "Optimized targeting" via campaign.optimized_targeting_enabled = False when you need tight audience control.
4. Set frequency cap at the campaign level: 5 impressions / user / week (default starting point).
5. Create at least one Ad Group with type=DISPLAY_STANDARD and a default CPC bid.
6. Add criteria to the ad group: at most 2 of {audience, topic, keyword, placement}. Use AdGroupCriterionService.mutate_ad_group_criteria.
7. Upload image assets via AssetService.mutate_assets with raw binary bytes (not URLs). One mutate per batch of up to 10 images.

8. Assemble a ResponsiveDisplayAd referencing the asset resource_names. Provide max counts: 5 headlines, 1 long_headline, 5 descriptions, 1 business_name, 5+ marketing_images, 5 square_marketing_images, 1 logo.
9. Attach the ad to the ad group via AdGroupAdService.mutate_ad_group_ads.
10. Verify budget, network_settings, optimized_targeting, frequency_cap by reading the campaign back with a SearchStream query before flipping status=ENABLED.
11. Wait 24-48h, then run the placement performance query and add NEGATIVE placement criteria for any URL with >100 impressions and 0 conversions.

section.name: contextual-keyword-targeting
section.p: Keyword targeting on Display uses BROAD match type only — phrase and exact match do not apply to display targeting. Keywords are used to find contextually relevant pages, not to match search queries.

antipattern.description: Using EXACT or PHRASE match types for display keyword targeting.
antipattern.reason: Display keyword targeting ignores match type qualifiers; only BROAD is effective. Setting other match types creates confusion without changing behavior.

section.name: placement-targeting
section.p: Placement targeting restricts delivery to specific URLs or app IDs. Use to target known high-performing sites or to exclude low-quality placements found in the performance report.

rule.statement: Review the placement performance report weekly and add low-CTR or high-cost/no-conversion placements as negative placements.
rule.rationale: Display campaigns without placement exclusions waste significant budget on app games, news aggregators, and parked domains with no purchase intent.

section.name: audience-targeting
section.p: Audience targeting uses user_list resource names. Sources: Custom Audiences (website visitors), In-Market Segments (users actively researching a product category), Affinity Segments (users with demonstrated interests), Customer Match (hashed contact data uploads).

rule.statement: Provide the maximum asset count Google allows: 5 headlines, 5 descriptions, 15 marketing images, 5 logos. More assets give the algorithm more combinations to test, improving performance over time.
rule.rationale: Google automatically tests asset combinations. Providing only the minimum (1 headline, 1 description, 1 image) eliminates the optimization surface and treats a responsive ad like a static ad.

list.item: Headlines: 1-5 required (max 30 chars each)
Long headline: 1 required (max 90 chars)
Descriptions: 1-5 required (max 90 chars each)
Business name: 1 required (max 25 chars)
Marketing images: 1-15 (1.91:1, min 600x314, recommend 1200x628)
Square marketing images: 1-15 (1:1, min 300x300, recommend 1200x1200)
Logo images: 0-5 optional (1:1 and/or 4:1, min 128x128 / 512x128)
Final URL: required

section.name: image-upload
section.p: Images must be uploaded as Asset objects before being referenced in a responsive display ad. The asset_service.mutate_assets() call returns a resource_name used in the AdImageAsset reference.

rule.statement: Upload images as binary content (requests.get(image_url).content), not as URLs. The API does not accept external image URLs directly in the asset payload.
rule.rationale: The Google Ads API requires binary image data in the asset creation request. Passing a URL causes a validation error without a clear error message.

section.name: performance-reporting
section.p: Query detail_placement_view for placement-level performance. Filter by campaign.id and date range. Sort by metrics.impressions DESC to identify top and bottom-performing placements.

commands.command: python -c "from google.ads.googleads.client import GoogleAdsClient; c = GoogleAdsClient.load_from_storage(); print(c.login_customer_id)"

example.title: Create a paused Display campaign
example.code:
from google.ads.googleads.client import GoogleAdsClient
client = GoogleAdsClient.load_from_storage() svc = client.get_service("CampaignService") op = client.get_type("CampaignOperation") c = op.create c.name = "DSP-2026-Q2-RDA" c.advertising_channel_type = client.enums.AdvertisingChannelTypeEnum.DISPLAY c.status = client.enums.CampaignStatusEnum.PAUSED c.manual_cpc.enhanced_cpc_enabled = False c.campaign_budget = "customers/{cid}/campaignBudgets/{bid}".format(cid=CID, bid=BID) ns = c.network_settings ns.target_google_search = False ns.target_search_network = False ns.target_partner_search_network = False ns.target_content_network = True c.frequency_caps.append({ "key": {"level": client.enums.FrequencyCapLevelEnum.CAMPAIGN, "event_type": client.enums.FrequencyCapEventTypeEnum.IMPRESSION, "time_unit": client.enums.FrequencyCapTimeUnitEnum.WEEK, "time_length": 1}, "cap": 5, }) c.optimized_targeting_enabled = False resp = svc.mutate_campaigns(customer_id=CID, operations=[op]) print(resp.results[0].resource_name)

example.title: Upload an image asset (binary, not URL)
example.code:
import requests from google.ads.googleads.client import GoogleAdsClient
client = GoogleAdsClient.load_from_storage() svc = client.get_service("AssetService") op = client.get_type("AssetOperation") a = op.create a.name = "marketing-image-1200x628-hero" a.type_ = client.enums.AssetTypeEnum.IMAGE a.image_asset.data = requests.get("https://cdn.example.com/hero.jpg", timeout=30).content resp = svc.mutate_assets(customer_id=CID, operations=[op]) asset_resource_name = resp.results[0].resource_name

example.title: Query placement performance (last 7 days)
example.code: ga = client.get_service("GoogleAdsService") query = """ SELECT detail_placement_view.target_url, detail_placement_view.placement_type, metrics.impressions, metrics.clicks, metrics.cost_micros, metrics.conversions, metrics.ctr FROM detail_placement_view WHERE segments.date DURING LAST_7_DAYS AND campaign.id = {cid} ORDER BY metrics.cost_micros DESC LIMIT 500 """.format(cid=CAMPAIGN_ID) for row in ga.search_stream(customer_id=CID, query=query): for r in row.results: ... # collect rows for exclusion logic

prompt.purpose: Generate RDA copy that fits Google character limits

content.prompt: You are a Google Ads RDA copywriter. Produce a JSON object matching this schema: { "headlines": [string x 5], # each <= 30 chars, no emoji, no exclamation spam "long_headline": string, # <= 90 chars "descriptions": [string x 5], # each <= 90 chars "business_name": string, # <= 25 chars "final_url": string # https URL } Constraints: - Do not include the brand name in every headline (Google penalizes repetition). - Each headline must stand alone (Google rotates them independently). - No discount/percentage claims unless explicitly provided in the brief. - All strings ASCII or single locale; no mixed-language pairs. Return ONLY valid JSON. If any string would exceed limits, truncate at a word boundary. Brief: <<<BRIEF>>>

list.item: Responsive Display Ads (RDA) auto-generate combinations — you cannot fully control which headline / image combo serves; brand teams hate this.
Image asset requirements are strict: marketing images 1.91:1 (1200x628 min), square 1:1 (1200x1200 min), logo 1:1 and 4:1. Wrong ratios silently rejected.
placement.url targeting accepts both domains and YouTube channel URLs; mixing them creates ambiguous criteria that don't fire as expected.
Display reach numbers in Keyword Planner are inflated; treat them as upper-bound only.
Audience-only targeting (no contextual signal) often delivers very low CTR; combining audience with topic and placement narrows fast.
View-through conversions inflate Display ROAS — agents that optimize on view-through often shift budget to vanity placements.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A Display agent should manage three loops: (1) creative refresh — periodically upload new image assets and rotate them into RDAs, (2) placement hygiene — daily query of detail_placement_view, exclude underperformers, (3) audience refresh — push updated remarketing lists. All three should produce a diff preview before committing. Pair with a "policy gate" check: validate image ratios and file size locally before calling AssetService to avoid noisy upload failures.

antipattern.description: Agents auto-generating ad copy often exceed the 30-char headline or 90-char description limit and the request fails server-side.
antipattern.reason: Character limits are strict; oversized strings are silently rejected with a generic validation error.

antipattern.description: Image asset uploads send raw bytes; agents that pass URLs without fetching them first hit AssetError.INVALID_IMAGE.
antipattern.reason: The Google Ads API requires binary image data in the asset creation request. Passing a URL causes a validation error without a clear error message.

antipattern.description: Models confuse placement (specific URL) with topic (Google's content category) — these are different criterion types.
antipattern.reason: Topics are Google's managed categories; placements are specific URLs or app IDs. Using the wrong type silently changes targeting.

antipattern.description: Customer Match list upload requires SHA-256 of normalized lowercase email; LLMs frequently skip the lowercase or strip step.
antipattern.reason: Email normalization (lowercase, strip whitespace) is mandatory; skipping it results in 0% match rate with no error message.

antipattern.description: Agents reading metrics.conversions may miss metrics.view_through_conversions; double-counting risk if both summed.
antipattern.reason: View-through conversions are tracked separately; summing both inflates ROAS calculations. Use conversions only unless analyzing display-specific metrics.

list.item: Enabling a Display campaign or raising budget by more than 25%
Removing an exclusion list or switching to "Optimized targeting"
Google's "Optimized targeting" auto-expansion can ignore your audience config and target whoever it wants; agents must explicitly disable it for tight-targeted campaigns
Combine targeting layers: audience AND topic, OR audience AND placement — pure broad audience burns budget.
Always upload at least 5 marketing images, 5 square images, 1 logo — fewer assets equals less rotation equals creative fatigue.
Set target_content_network = True and disable Search and Search Partner explicitly on Display campaigns; default settings can leak.
Build placement-exclusion lists at the account level (shared library) so every Display campaign inherits them automatically.
Use Customer Match and similar audiences for retargeting; raw cookie remarketing is degrading post-cookie deprecation.
Frequency cap (3-7 impressions per user per week) — Display without caps creates ad fatigue and brand annoyance fast.
Monitor placement_type in reports separately for WEBSITE, MOBILE_APPLICATION, YOUTUBE_VIDEO; mobile-app inventory often dominates spend with poor conversions.

checklist.check: Campaign created with advertising_channel_type=DISPLAY and status=PAUSED
network_settings: search=False, search_partner=False, content=True (verify by reading back)
optimized_targeting_enabled=False unless explicitly approved by human
Ad group has at most 2 targeting layers (audience+topic OR audience+placement)
All keyword criteria use BROAD match type
Image assets uploaded as binary bytes (requests.get(...).content), never URL strings
Image dimensions validated locally: 1.91:1 (≥600x314) and 1:1 (≥300x300) before AssetService call
RDA includes 5 headlines (≤30 chars), long_headline (≤90 chars), 5 descriptions (≤90 chars), business_name (≤25 chars)
Conversion tracking active and reported in metrics.conversions (NOT view_through_conversions)
Budget delta vs previous < 25% OR explicit human approval recorded

verify.step: 1. Read campaign back: SELECT campaign.network_settings.target_google_search, campaign.network_settings.target_content_network, campaign.optimized_targeting_enabled, campaign.frequency_caps FROM campaign WHERE campaign.id = {id} — assert search=False, content=True, optimized_targeting=False.
2. Validate RDA character limits locally before submit: all(len(h) <= 30 for h in headlines) and len(long_headline) <= 90 and all(len(d) <= 90 for d in descriptions) and len(business_name) <= 25.
3. Validate image dimensions and aspect ratio with Pillow before AssetService call: w,h = Image.open(BytesIO(b)).size; assert abs(w/h - 1.91) < 0.02 or abs(w/h - 1.0) < 0.02.
4. After 24h: query detail_placement_view; assert at least 100 impressions distributed across >5 placements (otherwise targeting is over-constrained).
5. Diff preview before any mutate: print operation.update_mask paths and operation.create JSON; require human acknowledgement for budget mutations >25%.
6. Confirm metrics.conversions is non-zero before scaling budget; never scale based on metrics.view_through_conversions.
7. Run weekly placement hygiene: any placement with cost_micros > 1_000_000 (1 USD) and conversions == 0 over 7 days → add as negative placement criterion.

warning.p: Never enable Optimized Targeting on a Display campaign that uses Customer Match remarketing. Google may expand to lookalike segments and bypass the curated list, producing irrelevant impressions billed at full CPC.

template.purpose: Asset dict structure for responsive display ad creation
